title: Project Initialization
description: Creating a new Composer project or bootstrapping an existing one via init and create-project commands

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: User invoking composer CLI commands to initialize or create projects

use_cases.name: Initialize New Package (composer init)
use_cases.description: Creates a basic composer.json in the current directory, interactively or via CLI options
use_cases.preconditions: No composer.json exists in current directory (or user wishes to overwrite)
steps: Call Factory::createConfig() if custom repositories provided
Call Factory::getComposerFile() to determine output path
Encode options to JSON via JsonFile::encode()
If interactive: display JSON preview, ask confirmation
If --autoload: create src/ dir via Filesystem::ensureDirectoryExists()
If no dependencies: run dump-autoload command
If interactive + .git exists: offer to add /vendor/ to .gitignore
If interactive + has dependencies: offer to run 'update' command
sub_flows: Interactive wizard (interact()): prompts for name, description, author, stability, type, license, require, require-dev, autoload path
Non-interactive (initialize()): auto-sets name from cwd+git config, author from git config

use_cases.name: Create Project from Package (composer create-project)
use_cases.description: Installs an existing Composer package as a new project into a target directory
steps: If --ask: interactively prompt for project directory
Call installProject(io, config, input, package, directory, version, ...)

use_cases.name: Install Root Package
steps: Derive target directory from package name if not specified
Add default or custom repositories via RepositoryFactory::defaultRepos() / configFromString()
Resolve best package candidate via VersionSelector::findBestCandidate()
Install root package via ProjectInstaller + InstallationManager::execute()

use_cases.name: Install Project Dependencies
steps: If --add-repository: write repo to composer.json via JsonConfigSource
If --require: add packages to composer.json via JsonConfigSource
If lock file exists and config changed: delete composer.lock
Configure: preferSource/Dist, devMode, platformFilter, optimizeAutoloader, auditConfig
Optionally remove VCS dirs (.git, .svn, etc.) via Filesystem::removeDirectory()

use_cases.name: Load/Create Configuration
use_cases.description: Bootstraps global Config object used by both init and create-project

use_cases.name: Create Composer Instance
use_cases.description: Full Composer bootstrap used internally by both commands
steps: Call createConfig() and merge local config/auth
Purge stale packages from local repository

key_relationships: InitCommand calls Application::find('update') / find('dump-autoload') for post-init
Factory::createComposer produces Composer object with all managers wired
include_extend_notes: UC1 <<include>> UC3 (Factory::createConfig always called if repositories option set)
UC2 <<include>> UC3 (Factory::createConfig always called in execute())
UC2 <<include>> UC2b (installProject always calls dep install unless --no-install)
UC1 <<extend>> 'Run dump-autoload' (only when --autoload set and no deps)
UC1 <<extend>> 'Install dependencies' (only interactive + hasDependencies + user confirms)
UC2b <<extend>> 'Remove VCS history' (only if installed from VCS and not --keep-vcs)
important_notes: InitCommand's interact() drives an interactive wizard collecting all package metadata before execute() writes the file. CreateProjectCommand::installProject() is the workhorse: it orchestrates package resolution, download, dependency install, VCS cleanup, and script dispatch. Factory is purely a service/factory class — not a command. Factory::createConfig is static; Factory::createComposer is an instance method (requires `new Factory()`). Both commands accept --repository to use custom package sources.
